entities.name: WESTERN RAJASTHAN
entities.description: Three districts in the western part of Rajasthan, India, where the study was conducted

entities.name: COMMON PROPERTY RESOURCES
entities.description: Shared resources in villages that have experienced decline in area and quality

entities.name: INSTITUTIONAL CHANGES
entities.description: Changes in village-level institutions associated with the decline of common property resources

entities.name: LAND REFORMS
entities.description: Policy changes introduced in the early 1950s leading to privatization and disruption of management systems

entities.name: PRIVATIZATION
entities.description: The process of converting common property resources into private ownership

entities.name: TRADITIONAL MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
entities.description: The historical system used to maintain and manage common property resources

entities.name: LIVESTOCK FARMING
entities.description: The practice of raising animals, which has seen increased commercialization

entities.name: TRACTORS
entities.description: Agricultural machinery whose increased use contributed to the decline of common property resources

entities.name: DEMOGRAPHIC PRESSURE
entities.description: Population growth that contributed to the deterioration of common property resources

entities.name: HERD SIZE
entities.description: The number of animals in a livestock herd, which farmers reduced in response to shrinking resources

entities.name: PRIVATE RESOURCES
entities.description: Resources owned privately that farmers rely on more as common resources shrink

entities.name: ARID REGION
entities.description: Dry climatic region where livestock farming typically has a comparative advantage

entities.name: SUBMARGINAL LANDS
entities.description: Low-quality lands transferred from grazing to cropping

entities.name: GRAZING
entities.description: The practice of livestock feeding on pasture, which decreased as lands were transferred to cropping

entities.name: CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing crops, which increased as submarginal lands were transferred from grazing

entities.name: RESOURCE DEGRADATION
entities.description: The deterioration of the environment, accelerated by the transfer of grazing lands to cropping

Increased use contributed to the decline of
Shrinkage led to increased reliance on
